summary: Bug fix verified: ArticleView.tsx 'Edit Article' button visibility now correctly restricted to platform_admin role only. Both test scenarios passed successfully.
passed_tests: TEST 1 PASSED: Platform admin (admin@automateaccounts.local, role=platform_admin) sees 'Edit Article' button (data-testid='article-edit-admin-btn') on article view at /articles/:id
TEST 2 PASSED: Tenant B admin (adminb@tenantb.local, role=partner_super_admin) does NOT see 'Edit Article' button on article view - only PDF and DOCX download buttons are shown
TEST 3 PASSED: data-testid='article-edit-admin-btn' is present only for platform_admin role, correctly absent for partner_super_admin
action_items: MINOR NOTE: Task description mentions partner code 'tenantb' for Tenant B, but actual partner code in database is 'tenant-b-test'. Update test documentation/credentials for accuracy.
critical_code_review_comments: ArticleView.tsx line 14: Fix confirmed correct - `const isAdmin = user?.role === 'platform_admin'` properly restricts Edit Article button to platform_admin only

test_credentials.tenant_b_admin: adminb@tenantb.local / ChangeMe123! (actual partner code: tenant-b-test, NOT 'tenantb')

context_for_next_testing_agent: Bug fix for Edit Article button visibility working correctly. Key facts: (1) partner code for Tenant B is 'tenant-b-test' NOT 'tenantb' as mentioned in task. (2) platform_admin role sees Edit Article button. (3) partner_super_admin role does NOT see Edit Article button. (4) Fix in ArticleView.tsx line 14: `const isAdmin = user?.role === 'platform_admin'`. Auth gateway data-testid selectors working: login-form, login-email-input, login-password-input, login-submit-button.
